__fallback__.headline: Offline potato guidance
__fallback__.next_step: Retake a clear potato leaf photo and confirm in the field before applying chemical treatment.
__fallback__.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
actions: Retake a close photo of one potato leaf in even light if symptoms are unclear.
Separate heavily affected foliage if it can be done safely.
Avoid broad spraying based only on the offline result.
monitoring: Check the same plants again within 24 to 48 hours.
Inspect nearby potato rows for similar lesions.
prevention: Clean tools before moving between rows.
Reduce long periods of leaf wetness where possible.
escalate_if: Symptoms spread quickly across several plants.
Stem collapse or tuber rot becomes visible.
notes: This is crop-specific offline guidance and still needs field confirmation.
__fallback__.verification_note: Draft treatment guidance. Expert agronomy review is required before pesticide use.

Potato___healthy.headline: Leaf appears healthy
Potato___healthy.next_step: No disease class was flagged. Continue routine scouting and rescan only if symptoms appear.
actions: Rescan only if new spots, blight, or yellowing begin to spread.
monitoring: Inspect lower leaves during the next field round.
Watch after wet or cool weather for fresh lesions.
prevention: Keep tools clean and remove badly damaged foliage promptly.
Maintain balanced irrigation and nutrition to reduce plant stress.
escalate_if: New lesions spread across several leaves.
Nearby plants begin to show the same pattern.
notes: Healthy predictions can still miss early field symptoms. Keep scouting.
Potato___healthy.verification_note: No pesticide treatment is recommended for a healthy result.

Potato___Early_blight.headline: Likely early blight
Potato___Early_blight.next_step: Verify soon and reduce leaf wetness. Remove the most affected lower leaves first if safe.
Potato___Early_blight.active_ingredient: Protectant fungicide such as mancozeb or chlorothalonil; rotate with locally registered systemic fungicides when disease pressure is high.
Potato___Early_blight.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for potato early blight control.
Potato___Early_blight.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Potato___Early_blight.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label PHI for potato.
Potato___Early_blight.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered fungicide label REI.
actions: Remove heavily damaged lower leaves where practical.
Keep foliage from touching wet soil if possible.
monitoring: Check lower leaves and neighboring plants within 2 days.
Watch lesion expansion after humid nights or rainfall.
prevention: Remove old infected crop debris after harvest where practical.
escalate_if: Defoliation starts moving up the canopy quickly.
New lesions appear across multiple rows.
notes: Confirm in the field before choosing fungicide chemistry.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Potato___Early_blight.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.

Potato___Late_blight.headline: Possible late blight
Potato___Late_blight.next_step: Treat as urgent. Isolate and verify immediately because spread can be rapid in cool, wet conditions.
Potato___Late_blight.active_ingredient: Late blight fungicide program using locally registered products such as azoxystrobin, cymoxanil mixtures, dimethomorph, propamocarb, or mefenoxam-based options, rotated with protectants.
Potato___Late_blight.dosage: Use the locally registered label rate for the chosen potato late blight product and program.
Potato___Late_blight.ppe: Gloves, mask or respirator as per label, eye protection, long sleeves
Potato___Late_blight.pre_harvest_interval: Follow the locally registered late blight product PHI for potato.
Potato___Late_blight.re_entry_interval: Follow the locally registered late blight product REI.
actions: Separate severely affected plants or rows if feasible.
Avoid moving wet infected foliage through the field.
Seek same-day confirmation if symptoms are spreading.
monitoring: Inspect twice daily in cool, wet weather.
Watch stems and nearby plants for rapid new lesions.
prevention: Reduce prolonged leaf wetness and improve airflow immediately.
Clean tools and footwear before moving to unaffected blocks.
escalate_if: New infections appear across many plants within 24 to 48 hours.
Stem collapse or tuber symptoms become visible.
notes: Late blight can move very quickly. Do not delay field verification.
Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
Potato___Late_blight.verification_note: Advisory treatment options only. Confirm the locally registered product label for dosage, PHI, and REI before spraying.
